form.title: SURVEY
form.subtitle: Organisational and managerial innovation and performance of the University of Ngaoundéré

intro.heading: Introduction for respondents
intro.text: This questionnaire is part of an academic research on organisational and managerial innovation and the performance of the University of Ngaoundéré.
intro.anonymous: Your answers are strictly anonymous and will be used solely for scientific purposes.
intro.no_wrong: There are no right or wrong answers: we invite you to express your personal perception.

scale.instruction: For each statement, please indicate your level of agreement using the following scale:

form.success: Thank you! Your response has been recorded successfully.
form.error: An error occurred. Please try again.

A.title: Section A – General information
A.subtitle: (This information is used for statistical purposes only)

options.EC: Lecturer-researcher at the University of Ngaoundéré
options.PA: Administrative staff at the University of Ngaoundéré
options.RA: Academic / administrative leader (Rector, VP, SG, Deans, Directors, HoD)
options.ET: Student at the University of Ngaoundéré

items.io1: The university has reorganised its structures to improve its functioning.
items.io2: Administrative procedures have been simplified compared to the past.
items.io4: The university has introduced new digital management tools.

items.im1: Leaders encourage new ways of managing the university.
items.im2: Managers adopt a participatory management approach.
items.im3: Important decisions are based on clear objectives.
items.im4: The university experiments with new human resource management practices.
items.im5: Leaders support innovative initiatives despite constraints.

items.g3: Operating rules are clear and well known.
items.g4: Communication between management and stakeholders is satisfactory.
items.g5: The university has sufficient autonomy in its internal decisions.

items.e1: I feel involved in the functioning of the university.
items.e2: I actively participate in institutional activities.
items.e3: I am willing to make extra efforts for the university.
items.e4: I identify with the university's objectives.
items.e5: I feel responsible for the overall performance of the university.

items.a1: The university learns from its past experiences.
items.a2: Mistakes are analysed to improve practices.
items.a4: Stakeholders are encouraged to suggest improvements.
items.a5: Training programmes genuinely help improve operations.

G.title: Section G – University performance (public value approach)

items.pa1: The quality of teaching is satisfactory.
items.pa4: Students' academic results are generally satisfactory.

items.po4: Users are generally satisfied with the services.

items.ps1: The university contributes to the development of society.
items.ps2: Graduates integrate well into the local job market.
items.ps3: The university meets the country's socio-economic needs.
items.ps4: Access to the university is relatively equitable.

items.pi1: The university enjoys a good image in society.
items.pi3: The university is perceived as useful to society.
items.pi4: The university is stable and institutionally credible.

comment.title: Final remark (optional)
comment.label: Do you have any comments or suggestions regarding the functioning or performance of your university?
